items.title: Bus driver may have had medical episode before crash that killed 61yo man

children.content: A passenger who was critically injured when a Transperth bus smashed into a tree in Perth's north dies in hospital, as police investigate whether the driver suffered a medical episode.

articles.title: Multiple people injured in bus crash, two critically, in Perth's north

children.content: A man and a woman suffer critical injuries and five other passengers are hurt as a Transperth bus crashes into a tree in Perth's north on Thursday morning.

image.altText: Police standing in front of a bus that is badly damaged after colliding with a tree

items.title: Another pandemic 'something we need to prepare for', WA's public health chief says

image.altText: WA Chief Health Officer Clare Huppatz speaks to a reporter

children.content: Clare Huppatz, the first woman to be appointed WA's chief health officer, takes up her role at a time when Ebola, diphtheria and the flu are public health concerns.

items.title: Biggest universal magnetic map unlocks new chapter of space research

children.content: Data gathered by scientists at the Square Kilometre Array has resulted in a new "map" detailing the magnetic web of our observable universe.

gallery.altText: A generated image shows cloud-like plumes of vibrant red and blue of varying brightness against a black background.

thumbnail.altText: A generated image shows cloud-like plumes of vibrant red and blue of varying brightness against a black background.

items.title: Testing underway after mass bird death in country WA

image.altText: Two pink and grey galahs sit on yellow sunflowers.

children.content: Authorities will investigate why 78 birds were found dead or dying in a wheatbelt town, with locals speculating the cause could be related to mouse baits.

items.title: Unlicensed chip factory lands WA's 'spud king' in hot water

children.content: Potato baron Tony Galati's business group has been fined $20,000 after it was found to have built an illegal Spuddies potato chip factory in WA's South West.

image.altText: A supermarket owner in his store.

items.title: Ex-teacher jailed for sexually abusing children he was babysitting

children.content: Kurt David Charlton, a former WA teacher, is sentenced for sexual offences against nine victims, who were all aged under 13 at the time.

image.altText: A man in an orange tee shirt smiles to the camera.

images.altText: Two women look at a share price board outside the ASX in Sydney.

articles.title: China warns Australia 55pc beef tariffs could apply within days

children.content: China is cracking down on beef imports and says it is ready to slap Australia with an additional 55 per cent tariff in the coming days.

image.altText: Cuts of beef sit displayed in a butcher.

articles.title: What happens when fuel excise cuts end on June 30?

children.content: Experts say consumers have 30 days until they pay more at the pump if no peace deal is finalised in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

image.altText: Five red petrol pumps in a bowser; one with a red handle, then a blue, a green, a dark blue and a black handle.

children.content: ASIC has told Senate estimates it has eight active contracts with KPMG, despite the company facing an audit scandal. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: ASIC tells parliament it has eight current contracts with KPMG, despite audit scandal

updates.title: Australia supports call for rogue AI freeze plan

items.title: Emissions drop again, fuelling hopes Australia's hit a tipping point

children.content: Emissions are at their lowest point since the COVID pandemic shut down the economy, dropping 2.1 per cent over the year to December 2025.

image.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset

items.title: Mary Fowler has crossed the threshold from athlete to cultural icon

children.content: Matildas star Mary Fowler is using her platform to share what matters to her, as she navigates becoming a truly global footballing superstar.

image.altText: Matildas player Mary Fowler leans on a brick pillar and smiles at the camera

items.title: Concerns raised as mining companies take local groundwater leaving towns 'vulnerable'

children.content: As a major miner seeks approval to extend its magnetite ore operations in regional Western Australia to 2048, shires are increasingly concerned about whether there's enough groundwater to go around.

gallery.altText: A tank protests a mining water licence application

thumbnail.altText: A tank protests a mining water licence application

items.title: Women say getting a long-acting reversible contraceptive has been the 'best decision'

children.content: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

image.altText: Young women leans on her kitchen bench, posing for photo

items.title: Need a mood boost? Here's why gardening might be worth a try

children.content: While gardening alone is not a treatment for mental illness, a growing body of evidence links it with higher levels of mental wellbeing and life satisfaction.

image.altText: Hands tending to a plant in a pot.

items.title: UN envoy intervenes in Woodside gas plant challenge

children.content: The federal government's 40-year extension of Woodside's North West Shelf gas project on the Burrup Peninsula could violate human rights, an independent United Nations expert warns.

A gas flame burns as the sun sets over Woodside's Karratha processing plant.

items.title: Looking for another show like Off Campus? Right this way

children.content: Hannah and Garrett's steamy love story might be on ice, but we've found more romance and yearning to tick your comfort-viewing boxes.

children.content: You too could learn the dangers of celebrating too soon if you underestimate this week's news quiz.

items.title: Doctor in tears over 'atypical' moles as social media craze causes concern

children.content: Skin specialists are sounding a warning about the potential cancer risks associated with unregulated peptides at the heart of a social media-driven beauty craze.

image.altText: Large moles on a young person's back.

items.title: The race against time to contain Ebola

children.content: Tensions are running high as officials fight to stop the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where aid workers tell the ABC they are already playing catch-up with the deadly disease.

image.altText: A baby held by its mother who wears a face mask reaches towards the camera.

items.title: The Perth choir giving people their voice back

children.content: In an unassuming building in Perth's north, the Second Chance Choir is bringing people together through music to help them put aside years of trauma, violence and substance abuse.

items.title: Olympic cash debate swirls ahead of swimming trials

children.content: Australian swimmer Shayna Jack has added her voice to growing calls for Olympic prize money, believing athletes are missing out on what they financially deserve.

image.altText: Swimmer finishes a race, goggles on forehead, looking at her time

items.title: 'A lot of confidence' for Ash Gardner ahead of T20 Cup

children.content: Ash Gardner will hit Australia's Twenty20 World Cup warm-up matches full of confidence after a half-century in the last of three informal hit-outs against South Africa.

items.title: Water Corp says maintenance of 54,000km of pipes a challenge amid complaints

children.content: For two years, residents in one of the driest parts of Western Australia have watched thousands of litres of water leaking from a Water Corp-owned tank.

items.title: Gold reserves grow at Kalgoorlie's Super Pit

children.content: A big exploration push by Australia's biggest gold miner, Northern Star Resources, has grown the reserves at the Super Pit by 54 per cent in the past six years.

image.altText: A mining truck carrying gold-bearing ore.

items.title: Murder victim tied up with plastic wrap, stomped in meth-fuelled burglary

children.content: Two men are convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith in his own home in Western Australia's South West during a meth-addled burglary for drugs.
